Outside, cold and blustery weather returned to Adams County after
several days of spring like temperatures. Inside, fragrant blossoms
and shiny green leaves on our greenhouse window plants and dwarf
citrus trees provide an antidote to the whipping winds. Nothing makes
a room smell more like spring than the soft lemony scent of the
blossoms of a Meyer lemon tree.
Dwarf Citrus Tree, Improved Meyer Lemon (Plant and Planter)
Children often wonder if they can grow a tree from the seeds found
inside oranges, tangerines, oranges, lemons, and grapefruit. Yes, as
long as the seed is not damaged, the odds are good that it will
produce a plant. But, you need to know that seedlings experience a
very long period before they flower and bear fruit- perhaps seven
years or more! Most citrus grown for indoor home use are dwarf
varieties that are either cuttings grown or grafted on a dwarfing
rootstock. By using cuttings or grafts from mature trees, a plant is
produced that is ready to begin fruiting right away.

Water: Your pot must have good drainage as you water every few days.
Mist the leaves and give your tree a shower occasionally.

column27: >We believe that transparency is fundamental to the trust you place in us. We are writing to inform you about a recent security incident we addressed and to let you know about upcoming system maintenance.
column28: >Our security team recently identified and resolved an incident involving unauthorized access to a portion of our internal server environment, where malware was discovered. Our team took immediate action to secure our systems and remove the threat.
column29: >As a precautionary measure to further strengthen our security protections, we will be performing essential updates to our server infrastructure. This maintenance will occur over the next few days and may result in brief, intermittent disruptions to your device. We are working to keep this disruption to an absolute minimum and apologize for any inconvenience this may cause.

column1: Enquiry on Integrations with Project Management SaaS,"Hello Customer Support, I am contacting you to seek information about the integrations compatible with your project management software. I would also like to know if your software provides APIs or webhooks for creating custom integrations. Could you give me a detailed list of all integrations and any associated documentation or resources for setting them up? Kelis’ milkshakes brought HIM to the yard?!,"Netflix restaurants, media monopolies and more. ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ The Culture Catchall Welcome to The Culture Catchall, where we discuss weekly pop culture moments that may have flown under your radar but that I’ve conveniently rounded up here. I’m your host, Ruth Etiesit Samuel, a culture reporter at HuffPost.After narrowly escaping a sepia-colored climate crisis, I’m back baby! And so is the 10th season of my personal guilty pleasure series “Love Island U.K.” (I’m several episodes behind, so no spoilers, please!)But let’s not overlook the chaotic end to Netflix’s “The Ultimatum: Queer Love,” with one final liveblog brought to you by Taryn Finley and Erin E. Evans. Whether you love or hate this format, get ready for more, because starting June 26, I and other members of the newsroom will be keeping up with Season 20 of ABC's “The Bachelorette.” Grab your popcorn and wine, and follow along with me on Monday nights. As for the current state of television, the WGA writers strike is still going on. Tony Awards host Ariana DeBose broke down how the strike leaves show presenters without the witty banter, jokes and teleprompters that make operations run smoothly. (Get into HuffPost’s coverage of the historic night here.) While the Directors Guild of America seemingly reached a tentative deal with the AMPTP, the WGA and SAG-AFTRA are standing their ground. By an “overwhelming margin,” 98% of the SAG-AFTRA members who voted have approved a strike if they can’t reach a deal with studios by June 30. I smell a double strike on the way…Speaking of networks, the BET Awards are right around the corner, airing Sunday, June 25, at 8 p.m. ET/PT. Though a host has yet to be announced, the show will be broadcast live from Microsoft Theater in Los Angeles. Certified lover boy and cornball Drake leads with seven award nominations, followed by Memphis rapper GloRilla with six. (This also means that we’re approaching the one-year anniversary of The Culture Catchall, woo!) Amid all the fanfare, Tyler Perry is rumored to have won the bid to buy BET, but rumors are just that — the news has not been confirmed.Glad to be back in the driver’s seat. Buckle up, folks, because we’ve got a lot of ground to cover. Here's the latest edition of The Culture Catchall. Read more We're Still Talking About It “Abbott Elementary” star Tyler James Williams is not playing into y’all’s latent queerphobia this Pride Month, OK?! Clearing up rumors about his sexuality, Williams talked about the dangers of overanalyzing someone’s actions, mannerisms, etc. and tethering it to their sexuality. After Williams shared his thoughts via some now-expired Instagram stories, his little brother chimed in, applauding him. HuffPost’s Jazmin Tolliver has all the heartwarming deets. The Recording Academy has seemingly heard our cries. The Grammy Awards will be undergoing some reform with the addition of three new categories: Best African Music Performance, Best Pop Dance Recording and Best Alternative Jazz Album. (Remember my gripe about an Afrobeats category? Let’s go ahead and add clairvoyance to my resume.) “African music” is a bit too broad for my liking, but this is a start, nonetheless. After the past few tumultuous years, the Hollywood Foreign Press Association will shut down — and be acquired by Dick Clark Productions and Todd Boehly’s investment company Eldridge. What does this mean? The Golden Globes will now be owned by Penske Media Corporation, which also owns Variety, Deadline, The Hollywood Reporter, Billboard, Rolling Stone and many other outlets. Is this a monopoly, or a pending antitrust lawsuit? Only time will tell. Well, Trump isn’t the only one facing time in the pen, chile. Tory Lanez’s sentencing has been delayed yet again
column2: this time, the judge pushed the date to Aug. 7. The Canadian rapper faces up to 22 years in prison for shooting Megan Thee Stallion in July 2020. According to Meghann Thee Reporter, his lawyers’ sentencing recommendation is due Aug. 1. Prosecutors have asked that Lanez be sentenced to 13 years in a state prison instead. The British royal family is back in the news. Prince Harry finally testified in court as a part of a phone-hacking lawsuit he leveraged against the tabloids. According to The Cut, four years ago, Diana’s younger son sued Mirror Group Newspapers for “allegedly hacking his phone and misusing private information to create salacious stories.” The “Spare” author’s comments on the stand and more can be found here. Read More Industry News & Announcements Janelle Monáe graces the cover of British Vogue’s Pride issue. Ke$ha won a years-long defamation case leveraged by producer Dr. Luke. Netflix is now embarking on opening restaurants, instead of lowering subscription prices. Alex Newell and J. Harrison Ghee made history as the first openly nonbinary actors to win Tonys. Track Olympian Tori Bowie died in May due to childbirth complications, per an autopsy. Kelis and Bill Murray are rumored to be dating. (Yes, you read that right.) Taylor Swift and “The 1975” frontman Matty Healy have reportedly broken up. NBC’s “Grand Crew” has been canceled after two seasons on the network. “I Love That For You” has been canceled by Showtime after one season. “All American: Homecoming” has been renewed for a third season at The CW. Netflix’s “They Cloned Tyrone,” starring Teyonah Parris, Jamie Foxx and John Boyega, drops July 21. For the first time in 30 years, no hip-hop album or song has hit No. 1 in 2023. “Black Panther” star Tenoch Huerta is facing accusations of abusive and sexually predatory behavior. Drew Barrymore lands the cover of New York magazine’s television issue. CNN CEO Chris Licht will be leaving the company after a startlingly brief time in the role, per Puck News. Insider News employees have now been on strike for 12 days after “steep layoffs.” Lindsay Lohan talks Dubai, pregnancy and her next chapter in the June cover story of Allure. Prime Video’s “The Summer I Turned Pretty” returns for Season 2 on July 14. “Spider-Man” star Shameik Moore is the cover star of Ebony’s June issue. Wade Goodwyn, longtime NPR newscaster, has died at the age of 63. Neil Everett, longtime “SportsCenter” anchor, is leaving ESPN after 23 years. “Vanderpump Rules” star Ariana Madix covers Glamour’s June issue. “Bling Empire” star Anna Shay, 62, died from a stroke on June 5, per People. Spotify is laying off 200 employees as part of “restructuring” its podcast division. Olivia Rodrigo plans to release “Vampire,” her first single since “Sour,” on June 30. Bronx rapper Ice Spice is Teen Vogue’s June cover star. Doja Cat finally reveals new music coming June 16, per Twitter. Edward Enninful will step away from British Vogue’s editor-in-chief position into a “global advisory role.” Padma Lakshmi is leaving “Top Chef” after 17 years as a host and producer on the show. It’s official: Tennis star Naomi Osaka is having a baby girl with rapper boyfriend Cordae. “Sex Lives of College Girls” star Renee Rapp announces new album “Snow Angel,” dropping Aug. 18. The Los Angeles Times has cut 74 newsroom positions due to “advertising declines.” “Bob's Burgers” star Jay Johnston has been charged by the DOJ for allegedly storming the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6. ""Bottoms,” starring Ayo Edebiri and Rachel Sennott, premieres in theaters on Aug. 25. From The Desk HuffPost’s Culture Desk has been up to a lot in the past few weeks. Here are some of the most recent happenings from my colleagues and me: Senior culture reporter Marina Fang reviewed the A24 film “Past Lives,” written and directed by the critically acclaimed playwright Celine Song. Starring Greta Lee, the romantic drama follows two former childhood friends who reconnect decades later for one week. Fang wrote about how the film’s comparisons to Richard Linklater’s “Before” trilogy don't quite do it justice — and why “Past Lives” will surprise you at every turn. Beware of minor spoilers ahead. Senior culture reporter Taryn Finley talked to hip-hop pioneer Yo-Yo for the latest installment of “I Run This,” a series spotlighting Black women and femmes making waves in culture and entertainment. Finley chatted with the Los Angeles-born rapper and actor about being Ice Cube’s protégée, reflecting on the 50th anniversary of hip-hop, and embarking on a new chapter as she launches her own cooking show. Dig in here, pun intended. Closing out Asian Pacific American Heritage Month, I profiled 20-year-old Aoki Lee Simmons, daughter of supermodel Kimora Lee Simmons and entertainment mogul Russell Simmons. Aoki, one of the youngest graduates in Harvard’s history, spoke to me about her plans after undergrad, navigating race and identity, and walking in her mother’s footsteps — literally. Get into it. As “Transformers: Rise of the Beasts” takes off in theaters, Black Voices editor Finley talked to actor Dominique Fishback about her remarkable year. The “Swarm” star talked to Finley about her East New York roots, her ability to fully immerse herself in different roles, and her goal to revolutionize film and acting. Learn more about Fishback’s burgeoning career here. Senior culture reporter Fang wrote about “Succession” creator Jesse Armstrong and his statement of solidarity in support of the writers strike. In an interview with NPR’s “Fresh Air with Terry Gross,” Armstrong explained how writers are striking over the very conditions that make shows like ""Succession"" possible. Read up here. The last week was pretty terrible (Olyvia's finger, Sam was quite sick, the time off work fell through, our car got broken into...) and I just couldn't get to it. I'm going to spend as much time as possible over the Christmas/New Year period working on it, and get you a completed draft mid January. If I can't make that, then I'm going to have to concede that I just can't get it done. Hope you & Lara & Lily have a good Christmas! Cheers, Tony",0,1
